title: मंगलवार व्रत कथा
devanagariText:
॥ श्री मंगलवार व्रत कथा ॥

ॐ श्री हनुमते नमः॥

॥ व्रत विधि ॥
मंगलवार का व्रत हनुमानजी को समर्पित है। प्रातःकाल स्नान करके हनुमानजी की मूर्ति या चित्र के सामने सिन्दूर, चमेली का तेल चढ़ाएँ। हनुमान चालीसा और बजरंग बाण का पाठ करें। एक समय भोजन करें। सायंकाल कथा सुनें।

॥ कथा ॥
प्राचीन काल में एक नगर में एक ब्राह्मण परिवार रहता था।
वह ब्राह्मण प्रतिदिन हनुमानजी की पूजा करता था।
प्रत्येक मंगलवार को व्रत रखता, हनुमानजी के मन्दिर में जाकर
सिन्दूर चढ़ाता और हनुमान चालीसा का पाठ करता था॥

उस ब्राह्मण की एक पुत्रवधू थी जो बहुत घमण्डी थी।
वह कभी हनुमानजी की पूजा नहीं करती थी
और मंगलवार के व्रत का उपहास करती थी।
वह कहती — इस व्रत से क्या होगा? यह सब अन्धविश्वास है॥

कुछ समय बाद उस परिवार पर संकट आने लगे।
धन-हानि हुई, स्वास्थ्य बिगड़ा और चारों ओर दुःख ही दुःख हो गया।
तब वह पुत्रवधू अपने ससुर के पास गई और पूछा —
क्या कारण है कि सब कुछ नष्ट हो रहा है?॥

ससुर ने कहा — बेटी, तूने हनुमानजी का अपमान किया है
और मंगलवार के व्रत का उपहास किया है।
बजरंगबली अत्यन्त शक्तिशाली और दयालु हैं।
जो उनकी भक्ति करता है उसकी सदा रक्षा होती है,
किन्तु जो अवहेलना करता है उसे कष्ट भोगना पड़ता है॥

पुत्रवधू को पश्चात्ताप हुआ। उसने तुरन्त मंगलवार का व्रत
प्रारम्भ किया। नियमपूर्वक हनुमानजी की पूजा की,
सिन्दूर चढ़ाया, हनुमान चालीसा पढ़ी।
धीरे-धीरे परिवार की स्थिति सुधरने लगी॥

हनुमानजी प्रसन्न हुए। उन्होंने स्वप्न में दर्शन देकर कहा —
मंगलवार का व्रत सदा श्रद्धापूर्वक करो।
मैं सदा तुम्हारी रक्षा करूँगा।
जो मेरा भक्त होकर मंगलवार का व्रत करता है,
उसके सभी संकट दूर होते हैं, शत्रु पराजित होते हैं
और परिवार में सुख-शान्ति रहती है॥

इसके बाद उस परिवार में फिर कभी दुःख नहीं आया।
सभी ने आजीवन मंगलवार का व्रत रखा
और हनुमानजी की कृपा से सुखी जीवन व्यतीत किया॥

॥ फलश्रुति ॥
जो भक्त श्रद्धापूर्वक मंगलवार का व्रत करता है
और इस कथा को सुनता है, उसके सभी संकट दूर होते हैं।
हनुमानजी की कृपा से भय, रोग, शत्रु और दरिद्रता का नाश होता है।
मंगल-ग्रह की पीड़ा शान्त होती है।

॥ इति श्री मंगलवार व्रत कथा सम्पूर्णा ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Mangalvar Vrat Katha ||

Om Shri Hanumate Namah.

|| Vrat Vidhi ||
Mangalvar Ka Vrat Hanumanji Ko Samarpit Hai. Pratahkal Snan Karke Hanumanji Ki Murti Ya Chitra Ke Samne Sindur, Chameli Ka Tel Chadhayein. Hanuman Chalisa Aur Bajrang Baan Ka Path Karen. Ek Samay Bhojan Karen. Sayankal Katha Sunen.

|| Katha ||
Prachin Kaal Mein Ek Nagar Mein Ek Brahman Parivar Rahta Tha.
Vah Brahman Pratidin Hanumanji Ki Puja Karta Tha.
Pratyek Mangalvar Ko Vrat Rakhta, Hanumanji Ke Mandir Mein Jakar
Sindur Chadhata Aur Hanuman Chalisa Ka Path Karta Tha.

Us Brahman Ki Ek Putravadhu Thi Jo Bahut Ghamandi Thi.
Vah Kabhi Hanumanji Ki Puja Nahin Karti Thi
Aur Mangalvar Ke Vrat Ka Upahasa Karti Thi.
Vah Kahti — Is Vrat Se Kya Hoga? Yah Sab Andhvishwas Hai.

Kuchh Samay Baad Us Parivar Par Sankat Aane Lage.
Dhan-hani Hui, Swasthya Bigda Aur Charon Or Duhkh Hi Duhkh Ho Gaya.
Tab Vah Putravadhu Apne Sasur Ke Pas Gayi Aur Puchha —
Kya Karan Hai Ki Sab Kuchh Nasht Ho Raha Hai?

Sasur Ne Kaha — Beti, Tune Hanumanji Ka Apman Kiya Hai
Aur Mangalvar Ke Vrat Ka Upahasa Kiya Hai.
Bajrangbali Atyant Shaktishali Aur Dayalu Hain.
Jo Unki Bhakti Karta Hai Uski Sada Raksha Hoti Hai,
Kintu Jo Avahelana Karta Hai Use Kasht Bhogna Padta Hai.

Putravadhu Ko Pashchattap Hua. Usne Turant Mangalvar Ka Vrat
Prarambh Kiya. Niyampurvak Hanumanji Ki Puja Ki,
Sindur Chadhaya, Hanuman Chalisa Padhi.
Dheere-dheere Parivar Ki Sthiti Sudharne Lagi.

Hanumanji Prasanna Hue. Unhone Svapna Mein Darshan Dekar Kaha —
Mangalvar Ka Vrat Sada Shraddhapurvak Karo.
Main Sada Tumhari Raksha Karunga.
Jo Mera Bhakta Hokar Mangalvar Ka Vrat Karta Hai,
Uske Sabhi Sankat Dur Hote Hain, Shatru Parajit Hote Hain
Aur Parivar Mein Sukh-shanti Rahti Hai.

Iske Baad Us Parivar Mein Phir Kabhi Duhkh Nahin Aaya.
Sabhi Ne Aajivan Mangalvar Ka Vrat Rakha
Aur Hanumanji Ki Kripa Se Sukhi Jivan Vyatit Kiya.

|| Phalashruti ||
Jo Bhakta Shraddhapurvak Mangalvar Ka Vrat Karta Hai
Aur Is Katha Ko Sunta Hai, Uske Sabhi Sankat Dur Hote Hain.
Hanumanji Ki Kripa Se Bhaya, Roga, Shatru Aur Daridrata Ka Nash Hota Hai.
Mangal-graha Ki Pida Shant Hoti Hai.

|| Iti Shri Mangalvar Vrat Katha Sampurna ||
hindiMeaning:
मंगलवार व्रत कथा में हनुमानजी की भक्ति और मंगलवार व्रत के प्रभाव का वर्णन है।

एक ब्राह्मण परिवार में ससुर नियमित हनुमान भक्त था किन्तु पुत्रवधू व्रत का उपहास करती थी। परिणामस्वरूप परिवार पर संकट आये। ससुर के बताने पर पुत्रवधू ने पश्चात्ताप करके मंगलवार व्रत प्रारम्भ किया।

हनुमानजी प्रसन्न होकर स्वप्न में प्रकट हुए और कहा कि मेरे भक्त के सभी संकट दूर होते हैं, शत्रु पराजित होते हैं और परिवार में सुख-शान्ति रहती है।

मंगलवार व्रत से भय, रोग, शत्रु, दरिद्रता का नाश होता है और मंगल ग्रह की पीड़ा शान्त होती है।
benefits: Traditional fasting story for Tuesday dedicated to Lord Hanuman
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of fears, enemies, and obstacles through hanuman's grace
Associated in tradition with diseases and protects family from misfortune
Pacifies afflictions of planet Mars (Mangal Graha)
Listening to the Katha with faith completes the vrat ritual
Traditionally associated with courage, strength, and victory in all endeavors
description: The Mangalvar Vrat Katha (Tuesday Fasting Story), known in Sanskrit as मंगलवार व्रत कथा, is a sacred prayer in praise of Lord Hanuman. Attributed to Hanuman Purana, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to pacify afflictions of planet Mars (Mangal Graha) as well as cure diseases and protect family from misfortune. Additionally, it is known to bring courage, strength, and victory in all endeavors. For best results, devotees chant this on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and during Hanuman Jayanti. At 12 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 10 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Mangalvar Vrat Katha (Tuesday Fasting Story) in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Mangalvar Vrat and who is it dedicated to?
seoFaqs.answer: Mangalvar Vrat is the Tuesday fasting observance dedicated to Lord Hanuman, who in Hindu tradition is regarded as the adhi-devata (presiding deity) of Graha Mangal — the planet Mars. The vrat is undertaken to invoke Hanuman's protection, to pacify Mars-related afflictions in one's natal chart (Manglik dosha), to overcome enemies, fears and obstacles, to seek strength and courage, and to fulfil specific desires such as childbirth, marriage of a daughter, healing of illness, or success in litigation. The vrat is observed weekly on Tuesdays, traditionally for a continuous span of 21 Tuesdays followed by a formal udyapan (concluding ceremony), though many devotees observe it lifelong. (Sources: drikpanchang.com Mangalvar Vrat; Hanuman Bhakti tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the procedure (vidhi) for observing Mangalvar Vrat?
seoFaqs.answer: The vidhi: rise early before sunrise, bathe and wear clean clothes (preferably red or saffron). Set up a small altar with a Hanuman murti or picture. Apply sindoor and chameli (jasmine) oil to the murti — this is the most important offering for Hanuman. Light a ghee or sesame-oil lamp, offer red flowers (especially red hibiscus or red roses), boondi laddoo or jaggery as naivedya. Recite Hanuman Chalisa, Bajrang Baan, Sundara Kanda excerpts and then read the Mangalvar Vrat Katha. Eat only one meal during the day, traditionally taken in the evening after the katha is read, consisting of wheat-based food (chapati, halwa) and abstaining from salt, onion, garlic and tamasic foods. Many devotees take only fruits and milk through the day. (Sources: Hanuman Vrat traditions; drikpanchang.com Tuesday vrat.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the central story (katha) of the Mangalvar Vrat?
seoFaqs.answer: The traditional katha tells of a Brahmin family in which the head of the household was a sincere Hanuman devotee who observed the Tuesday vrat with full devotion every week, while his daughter-in-law (putravadhu) was proud and dismissive — she ridiculed the vrat as superstition and refused to participate. As a result of her irreverence, the family began to suffer financial loss, illness and constant misfortune. When she questioned the cause, her father-in-law explained that her disrespect to Bajrangbali had brought these troubles. Repenting, she began the Mangalvar vrat with full faith, performing Hanuman puja with sindoor and reciting Hanuman Chalisa. The family's circumstances soon began to improve. Hanuman appeared in her dream, blessed her, and assured her that whoever observes the vrat with devotion will have all troubles removed, enemies defeated, and the family blessed with peace and prosperity. (Sources: traditional Mangalvar vrat katha texts; Hanuman Purana excerpts.)

seoFaqs.question: How long should one observe the Mangalvar Vrat for full benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: The most widely prescribed duration is 21 consecutive Tuesdays, after which a formal udyapan (concluding ceremony) is performed in which 21 brahmins or 21 Hanuman devotees are fed, donations of red items (red cloth, masoor dal, jaggery, copper) are offered, and a yajna or extended Hanuman Chalisa parayana (108 recitations) is conducted. Some devotees extend the vrat to 45 Tuesdays, 108 Tuesdays, or commit to it lifelong. Once the vrat begins, it must be observed without break — if a Tuesday is missed due to genuine illness, travel or impurity, the count traditionally restarts from the beginning. The first Tuesday of the bright fortnight (Shukla paksha) of any month, especially Chaitra, Margashirsha or Margashirsha Mangalvar Vrat, is the most auspicious starting point. (Sources: Mangalvar Vrat Kalpa; Hanuman Vrat Vidhi manuals.)

seoFaqs.question: What food restrictions must be observed during Mangalvar Vrat?
seoFaqs.answer: On Tuesdays, the vrat-observer takes only one meal during the day — traditionally in the evening after sunset and after the puja-katha is completed. The meal must be free of salt (namak), onion, garlic, eggs, meat, fish, and any tamasic substances. Common permitted foods include wheat-based items (chapati, puri, sweet halwa), gur (jaggery) instead of refined sugar, milk, fruits, dried fruits and ghee. Some strict observers take only fruits, milk or jaggery-water through the entire day (phalahara vrat). Red foods such as masoor dal, red apples, pomegranate are particularly auspicious. Black sesame, urad dal, brinjal, and pungent foods are avoided as they are associated with Shani rather than Mangal. (Sources: traditional vrat-mimamsa; ayurvedic Pitta-dosha guidelines for Mars-related vrats.)

seoFaqs.question: Who should specifically observe Mangalvar Vrat?
seoFaqs.answer: The vrat is especially recommended for: persons with Manglik dosha (Mars in 1st, 4th, 7th, 8th or 12th house) seeking marital harmony; unmarried persons facing delays in marriage due to Mangal placement; couples experiencing marital conflict, separation or in-law troubles; individuals with afflicted or debilitated Mars in their natal chart; persons running Mangal mahadasha or antardasha; those suffering from blood-related disorders, accidents, surgical complications, or anger management issues; people seeking courage, victory in litigation, or relief from enemies. It is also recommended for those born under Mesha or Vrishchika rashi, and for anyone seeking Hanuman's general protection from fears, ghosts, black magic, or Shani-related troubles. (Sources: classical jyotisha parihara texts; Hanuman Bhakti traditions.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the katha promise to those who observe the vrat?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti of the katha promises that sincere observance with faith brings: removal of all sankat (calamities) and fears; victory over enemies (shatru-parajay); peace and harmony in the family (parivar mein sukh-shanti); cure of diseases (roga-nashan); destruction of poverty (daridrata-nash); pacification of Mangal-graha pida (Manglik dosha relief); protection from fire, weapons, accidents and untimely death; blessing of progeny; and Hanuman's continuous unseen protection over the household. The story makes clear that these benefits flow not from mechanical observance but from sincere faith (shraddha) — the daughter-in-law's transformation from skeptic to devotee is the real teaching of the vrat. (Sources: katha phalashruti; Hanuman Bhakti commentaries.)
